avoid.market: Will the Fed cut rates 0 times? (KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0)
avoid.reason: Passes volume/spread filters but no verifiable non-fabricated real-time source for July 2026 FOMC path, dots, or incoming inflation/labor prints; liquid market (~81% no-cut) leaves no grounded edge ≥8%.

avoid.market: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027? (KXRATECUT-26DEC31)
avoid.reason: Complement of zero-cut contract; same lack of grounded July-2026 macro source; spread and efficiency make sub-threshold edge unreliable.

avoid.market: Will there be a recession in 2027? (KXRECSSNBER-27)
avoid.reason: Long-dated binary with wide outcome uncertainty; no specific BEA/NBER-consistent forecast source available without fabrication; edge not demonstrable at ≥8%.
Volume below $50K filter (max ~48K on T2.5); also advance-estimate edge requires data not in prompt.

avoid.market: All CPI YoY / monthly July 2026 thresholds (KXCPI*)
avoid.reason: Volume below $50K filter; BLS print not yet known and no external retrieval allowed.
Volume below $50K; same-day weather requires live NWS/model guidance not supplied; no fabricated forecast.
Volume below $50K on all names; spot/path edge needs live prices and term structure not in prompt.
Volume far below $50K; seasonal outlook edge not grounded without CSU/NOAA sources in prompt.

notes: Only three contracts clear volume≥$50K and spread≤0.10 (Fed zero-cuts, any 2026 cut, 2027 recession). Market-implied regime is neutral-to-mildly sticky: ~81% prob of zero 2026 cuts, CPI mass near 3.2–3.4%, Q2 GDP centered ~2%, 2027 recession ~42%. Without retrievable July-2026 data releases or live quotes, no non-fabricated grounding source exists, so edge thresholds cannot be met. Zero trades is the correct output.
